rationale_for_baseline_tier: Uncomplicated recurrent herpes labialis is benign and self-limiting; offer early antiviral and self-care with routine follow-up (T1). Ocular HSV and eczema herpeticum/erysipelas/SJS carry hard T4 safety-nets.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Eye becomes red, painful or sensitive to light, or blisters appear near the eye
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — urgent eye care / ED
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your eye becomes red, painful or sensitive to light, or you get blisters near your eye — get urgent eye care or go to ED that day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rapidly spreading hot red facial swelling, widespread blisters, mouth/skin peeling or target lesions, or feeling systemically unwell/feverish
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests erysipelas/cellulitis, eczema herpeticum or SJS — dermatological emergencies.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get spreading hot red facial swelling, widespread blisters, peeling in the mouth or skin, or feel really unwell — go to the Emergency Department that day.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Provide clear eye and spreading-infection red flags for self-monitoring.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Start the antiviral if you can within the first day or two, keep the lip moisturised, wash your hands and avoid touching your eyes. It should crust and heal over 7–10 days.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care + antiviral script; urgent eye care/ED for eye or spreading-infection features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Urgent eye care/ED for any eye symptoms at any time.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Recurrent herpes labialis; ocular and eczema-herpeticum/erysipelas/SJS screens negative; early antiviral offered; consider suppression if frequent.

critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Autoinoculation herpetic keratitis is missed and scars the cornea, damaging vision.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for an uncomplicated cold sore — unnecessary.
